task_completion.description: Open the target landscape image in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application is open and displays a landscape photograph (e.g., nature, mountains).

task_completion.description: Select a rectangular area in the upper-right portion of the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rectangle Select Tool is active, and a dashed selection outline ('marching ants') is visible in the upper-right quadrant (top 60%) of the image.

task_completion.description: Delete the selected rectangular area to make it transparent
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected rectangular area in the upper-right is empty and displays a checkerboard pattern, indicating true transparency (not just a solid white or colored block).

task_completion.description: Preserve the non-deleted content of the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rest of the landscape image outside the deleted rectangle remains completely visible and unmodified.

task_completion.description: Save or export the modified image
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog is visible, or there is evidence (like a file manager view or terminal command) showing the image being saved/overwritten to preserve the transparent cutout.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical interface to perform the edits
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GIMP application window with tools being used, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) bypassing the GUI.

integrity.description: The deleted area is genuinely transparent
integrity.visual_evidence: The deleted area shows GIMP's standard checkerboard pattern indicating an alpha channel/transparency, not a solid color block painted over the image.

integrity.description: Only the requested rectangular area was deleted
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no other random transparent holes, painted areas, or unintended modifications anywhere else in the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires editing a real landscape image (landscape_rect.jpg) using GIMP. The goal is to delete a rectangular area in the upper-right portion (within the top 60% of the image). The deleted area must be transparent (showing GIMP's checkerboard pattern), not a solid background color, and must be at least 50x50 pixels in size. The rest of the landscape image must remain completely unmodified.
